front: Novel: Jake Donaghue kidnaps movie star dog Mr. Mars
Novel: protagonist adapts conversations with Hugo Belfounder into The Silencer
Novel: retired playwright Charles Arrowby obsesses over first love Mary Hartley Fitch at Shruff End; Booker Prize winner
Novel: Toby Gashe finds a bell at the bottom of a lake near Imber Court
Novel: prospective nun Catherine Fawley driven mad at a lay religious community
Novel: Bradley Pearson convicted of Arnold Baffin's murder after falling in love with Baffin's daughter Julian
Novel: ends with 'Post-Scripts' by characters offering their own versions of events
Author: suffered from Alzheimer's; husband John Bayley wrote Elegy for Iris
Author: last novel Jackson's Dilemma showed vocabulary decline from Alzheimer's
Author: wrote The Sovereignty of Good, reviving Platonism in analytic moral philosophy
Novel: Sammy Starfield and Sadie Quentin plot to steal protagonist's manuscript; features translation of Le Rossignol de Bois
Novel: protagonist searches for ex Anna Quentin in Paris on Bastille Day
Novel: dedicated to Raymond Queneau, author of Zazie in the Metro
Novel: protagonist takes Beckett's Murphy when evicted from Madge's flat
Novel: Palmer Anderson steals Martin Lynch-Gibbon's wife Antonia; nearly every heterosexual pairing occurs
Author: drew on Simone Weil's prioritization of moral 'attention' over will
Author: The Fire and the Sun defends Plato's banishment of the poets
Author: favorite painting was Titian's Apollo Flaying Marsyas
Author: had affair with Elias Canetti, documented in his memoir Party in the Blitz
Novel: historian Lucas accidentally hits man with baseball bat trying to murder his brother
Novel: Titus drowns; retired playwright blames the husband of his childhood crush
